analysis: The broad concept 'rumor_vs_silence' conflates several distinct semantic dimensions. It lumps together loudness, type of noise, rhythmic patterns, and perceived source location.  A single vector representing 'rumor' is therefore inaccurate because it doesn't differentiate between, for example, a loud, erratic, close-range noise versus a quiet, steady, distant one. Decomposing the space into these sub-concepts allows for a more nuanced understanding of what constitutes 'rumor' versus 'silence', enabling more precise semantic representation and generation.

sub_concepts.description: Captures the perceived volume or amplitude of the sound. A loud sound is easily noticeable and potentially disruptive, while a quiet sound is subtle and easily missed.
sub_concepts.why_distinct: Distinguishes between sounds that are inherently loud versus those that are quiet, independent of their nature (e.g., a loud whisper vs. a quiet explosion).
pos_examples: Der Bühnentechniker: ein ständiges Brummen vom Kompressor, in Wellen, ohne Pause
La estudiante percibió un estruendo repentino de la multitud
Die Mutter nahm wiederholte harte Schläge von den Schienen wahr
neg_examples: Bibliothecaria: omne strepitum minimum erat quia spelunca omne echo absorbebat
Il cacciatore notò che la stanza restava ferma
La espeleóloga notó que el pasillo parecía vacío

sub_concepts.description: Focuses on the *kind* of sound. Disruptive noises are those that interfere with focus or communication, while ambient sounds are background noises that don't necessarily cause disturbance.
sub_concepts.why_distinct: Separates sounds based on their effect, not just their volume. A quiet, constant hum can be disruptive, while a loud but brief sound might be less so.
pos_examples: pectus vibrare faciebat, quasi malleus procul
neg_examples: un leises Rascheln wurde zum Ereignis

sub_concepts.description: Describes the temporal structure of the sound. Erratic sounds have unpredictable or irregular patterns, while steady sounds are consistent and predictable.
sub_concepts.why_distinct: Accounts for the impact of unpredictable sounds, which can be more jarring than constant ones, even if the latter are louder.
pos_examples: wie ein Hammer in der Ferne

sub_concepts.description: Indicates the perceived distance of the sound source. Distant sounds often feel less immediate and can be perceived as less threatening, while proximal sounds are closer and potentially more alarming.
sub_concepts.why_distinct: Acknowledges that the perceived impact of a sound is affected by its apparent location. A distant loud noise might be less concerning than a nearby quiet one.
pos_examples: wie ein Hammer in der Ferne
neg_examples: Der Bühnentechniker: ein ständiges Brummen vom Kompressor
La estudiante percibió un estruendo repentino de la multitud
Die Mutter nahm wiederholte harte Schläge von den Schienen wahr
